title: Reproducible subgroup-metric harness (open de-identified eval data only)
context: The bias-005 standard requires that subgroup metrics are either cited (reported-unverified) or independently reproduced (reproduced) on open eval data. Without a harness, only the authors' numbers can be cited, and bias gaps cannot be independently verified. The harness recomputes metrics only on open-access/de-identified eval data in a pinned, containerized environment, committing only aggregate results. It must never touch controlled-access or identifiable patient data. Depends on bias-005 and validator-006.
objective: Build and ship a reproducible subgroup-metric harness that recomputes reported metrics only on open-access/de-identified eval data in a pinned, containerized environment, commits only aggregate metrics and provenance pointers, and verifies subgroup breakdowns for at least one real delivered model card.
acceptanceCriteria: Harness recomputes reported metrics only on open-access/de-identified eval data via a pinned, containerized environment (image hash recorded in specVersions); it never downloads or touches controlled-access/identifiable patient data, and stops with a clear error if a controlled-access barrier or PII signal is encountered.
A committed synthetic eval fixture with known ground-truth subgroup metrics is reproduced exactly by the harness in CI; this golden fixture uses no real patient data.

At least one real delivered model card's subgroup metric is verified by the harness on open eval data; the result is written back to the card with verified:reproduced and provenance pointer committed.
The harness commits only aggregate metrics and provenance pointers (no patient images, rows, or individual-level data) to any tracked file.
Code is MIT-licensed; pnpm build && pnpm test && pnpm lint pass; no credentials or patient data in any committed file.
resources: PLAN.md — Reproducible subgroup-metric harness, Model & eval-data access protocol, Test fixtures & golden files
TCIA collection licenses (CC-BY open eval data)
output: A merged PR adding a reproducible subgroup-metric harness with a synthetic golden fixture (CI-verified), at least one real card metric verified with verified:reproduced, and no patient data committed.
